figures.gloss: An 'energy personality essence no longer focused in physical matter,' channeled by Jane Roberts. Seth provided a comprehensive metaphysical system detailing multidimensional reality, simultaneous time, and the mechanics of soul evolution.
figures.facet: Unlike spirit guides that offer moralizing comfort, Seth acts as a rigorous metaphysical professor, dismantling linear time and objective reality in favor of a purely psychological cosmos where belief precedes physical manifestation.
figures.provenance: The 'Seth Material' (1970) and 'The Nature of Personal Reality' (1974) by Jane Roberts and Robert Butts.

figures.gloss: A 35,000-year-old Lemurian warrior-conqueror who ascended into spirit, channeled by J.Z. Knight. He teaches quantum mysticism, self-empowerment, and the realization of one's own innate divinity, often through intense, physically demanding disciplines.
figures.facet: Ramtha blends prehistoric pulp-fantasy lore with aggressive self-deification. He eschews the gentle tones of typical New Age guides, appearing as a hyper-masculine warrior commanding followers to master reality through force of will.
figures.provenance: Channeled by J.Z. Knight since 1977; established the Ramtha's School of Enlightenment in 1988.

figures.gloss: The inner dictation received by Helen Schucman, universally identified by its practitioners as Jesus Christ. It delivers a rigorous, non-dualistic curriculum aimed at dismantling the ego and recognizing the physical world as a complete illusion.
figures.facet: It actively subverts traditional Christian theology using Christian vocabulary, recasting sin as a mere 'mistake' and the crucifixion as a pedagogical demonstration of the body's unreality, entirely removing divine punishment.
figures.provenance: 'A Course in Miracles' (1976), dictated by Helen Schucman and co-scribed by William Thetford.

figures.gloss: The central axiom of the channeling current: physical experience is the direct, unavoidable manifestation of conscious and subconscious beliefs. The universe is a mirror reflecting internal psychological states, with no independent material existence.
figures.facet: It completely eliminates victimization, chance, and external divine judgment. Every illness, tragedy, and triumph is seen as a self-authored event chosen by the soul for its own expansion and learning.
figures.provenance: First popularized by Jane Roberts in 'The Nature of Personal Reality' (1974); foundational to the broader New Age movement.

figures.gloss: The act of deliberately yielding one's vocal cords and physical body to a non-physical entity. This ranges from light intuitive dictation to full-body, deep-trance overshadowing with dramatically altered voices and mannerisms.
figures.facet: Unlike traditional shamanism, the channeler rarely undertakes soul-flight to other realms; instead, the physical body becomes an instrument, a localized broadcasting tower for disembodied intelligence to lecture the modern world.
figures.provenance: Descended from 19th-century Spiritualist mediumship, transitioning from physical phenomena (ectoplasm, rapping) to pure psychological and philosophical discourse.

figures.gloss: The greater 'Entity' from which individual human personalities are projected into physical time and space. It simultaneously experiences multiple lifetimes across past, present, future, and alternate probabilities, serving as the true, immortal self.
figures.facet: It fragments the concept of a single, linear soul. The human ego is merely one probe among many sent into physical reality by the Oversoul, fundamentally altering traditional concepts of reincarnation.
figures.provenance: Articulated deeply in the Seth Material; draws on Transcendentalist terminology but adds simultaneous time mechanics.

figures.gloss: The teaching that the physical universe is a hallucinatory construct or a dream of separation from the Source. In ACIM, matter is an error of the ego; in Seth, it is a creative camouflage.
figures.facet: It renders the material world theoretically infinitely malleable. Because matter is seen as solidified thought or a projection of guilt, salvation and manifestation rely entirely on changing the mind, never the world.
figures.provenance: Central to 'A Course in Miracles' and widespread in New Age channeling; parallels Hindu Advaita Vedanta and Gnostic thought.

figures.gloss: A self-described 'group consciousness' of non-physical teachers, channeled by Esther Hicks (with husband Jerry Hicks) since the mid-1980s at seminars and in books co-credited to 'Esther and Jerry Hicks.' Abraham teaches the 'Law of Attraction': that emotional 'vibrational' alignment, not effort, determines what manifests in a person's life.
figures.facet: Abraham commercially mainstreamed the tradition's core axiom for a mass audience — it was Abraham's material, not Seth's, that Rhonda Byrne's The Secret (2006) popularized worldwide, though later editions dropped the Hicks segments after a licensing dispute.
figures.provenance: Esther and Jerry Hicks, Ask and It Is Given (2004) and seminar recordings from the mid-1980s onward; coverage of The Secret (2006) documents the Law of Attraction crossover.

figures.gloss: A 'non-physical entity' who describes himself as having never incarnated in physical form, channeled by Jach Pursel since 1974. Lazaris became a fixture of the 1980s workshop circuit, teaching emotional processing and reality-creation technique to large seminar audiences through Concept: Synergy Inc.
figures.facet: Unlike Ramtha's warrior bravado or Seth's professorial systematizing, Lazaris presents as a gentle, explicitly never-incarnated teacher — evidence the tradition's entities need no claimed human backstory at all to draw a following.
figures.provenance: Jach Pursel, channeling since 1974 (public sessions via Concept: Synergy, founded with Peny Pursel); Lazaris material collected in seminar recordings and books such as the Lazaris Interviews series.

figures.gloss: An extraterrestrial of the 'Essassani' hybrid civilization, channeled by Darryl Anka since 1983; Bashar describes himself as an emissary from a parallel timeline making contact through the channeling medium, teaching that following one's 'highest excitement' collapses the most resonant of many probable realities into experience.
figures.facet: Bashar is the clearest merger of the channeling current with UFO-contactee culture — the entity is not a disembodied human spirit or an abstract energy-personality but a presently-living alien, reframing channeling as interdimensional contact rather than mediumship of the dead.
figures.provenance: Darryl Anka, channeling since 1983; Bashar: Blueprint for Change (1990) compiles early sessions.

figures.gloss: An entity describing itself as 'of magnetic service,' channeled by Lee Carroll since 1989; Kryon's books, beginning Kryon Book One: The End Times (1993), teach that Earth's magnetic grid is being deliberately retuned to support accelerated human spiritual evolution, alongside teachings on 'Indigo' and 'Crystal' children.
figures.facet: Kryon represents the current's institutionalized long tail — three-plus decades of continuous international conferences and channeled sessions, outlasting the 1980s seminar-circuit entities it followed.
figures.provenance: Lee Carroll, channeling since 1989; the Kryon book series beginning 1993.

figures.gloss: The impersonal wellspring — variously described as the 'Universal Forces' or the Akashic Records — that Edgar Cayce (1877-1945), the 'Sleeping Prophet,' claimed to consult in self-induced trance for over 14,000 recorded 'readings' (1901-1944) addressing medical diagnoses, past-life history, and cosmology.
figures.facet: Cayce's trance-reading format — a lay subject speaking in an altered state while an associate transcribes verbatim — is the acknowledged American template the entire post-1963 channeling current inherits, predating Seth and Ramtha by six decades.
figures.provenance: The Edgar Cayce readings (1901-1944), archived by the Association for Research and Enlightenment (A.R.E., founded 1931, Virginia Beach); Thomas Sugrue, There Is a River (1942).

edges.note: Intra-tradition bond: The trance is the literal biological mechanism by which the Seth personality was transmitted into physical reality.
Intra-tradition bond: The Oversoul utilizes the mechanism of conscious creation to experience itself across multidimensional realities and timelines.
Intra-tradition bond: The Voice systematically deconstructs the physical world as a dream of separation, making illusion its core pedagogical focus.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Helen Schucman explicitly bridged her channeled text to traditional Christianity by identifying the voice as the historical Jesus Christ.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both rely on an individual voluntarily entering a trance to allow spirits or deities to physically speak through their vocal cords.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both traditions operate on the premise that disembodied forces can temporarily indwell, possess, and animate a human vessel.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both serve as disembodied, transcendent pedagogues transmitting an intricate metaphysical cosmology to human scribes.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both conceptualize an invisible, animating mental or spiritual force that directly shapes and manipulates physical circumstances.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The ACIM concept of the ego functions similarly to the Gnostic demiurge, acting as the false creator of a flawed, illusory physical universe.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both represent the psychological force of illusion that uses fear and physical distraction to keep the mind trapped in the dream of matter.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both teach that the human personality contains an uncorrupted, transcendent fragment of a much larger, divine source.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The multidimensional entity mirrors the Kabbalistic Infinite, projecting localized fragments of itself into the lower physical spheres to experience manifestation.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both philosophies assert that consciousness is the fundamental bedrock of existence, and that matter is merely its localized projection.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both are primordial, ancient kings associated with a lost golden age who established metaphysical paths to immortality.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both act as cosmic scribes and teachers of hidden wisdom, dictating the underlying laws of the universe to humanity.
Deane's 'translation' of Guardian transmissions is received inner dictation in all but name — the tradition's own refusal of the channel label marks how close the mechanism runs.
The same attested practice and era — deep-trance vocal channeling in the American milieu of Seth and Ramtha; L/L Research names its own work channeling, adding an experimental discipline to it.
The same event on both sides of the seam: the channeling shard holds the dictating Voice; this shard holds the scribe, the curriculum, and the community it produced.
Abraham's 'Law of Attraction' is the seminar-circuit's most commercially successful application of the tradition's axiom that belief/vibration precedes manifestation.
Cayce's self-induced trance readings (from 1901) are the acknowledged American template for the deep-trance mechanism the whole post-1963 current inherits.
Both anchor the same 1970s-80s seminar-circuit peak of the channeling current, though Lazaris claims no past incarnation at all against Ramtha's specific warrior backstory.

Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: both are channeled extraterrestrial voices claiming benevolent guidance for humanity's evolution — Bashar as a named individual entity, Ashtar Command as a collective command structure.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: both are non-human 'guardian' teacher-entities claiming stewardship of humanity's spiritual evolution, channeled to a devoted following.

Speculative milieu parallel only: both are early-to-mid-20th-century American trance/celestial-dictation cosmologies predating the tradition's 1963 start date; no direct link between Cayce and the Urantia contact is documented.
Both are trance-channeling circuits of the same American esoteric milieu and decade, though Ra's is a deliberately 'tuned' three-person apparatus rather than a single channel working alone.
The channeled Voice is the claimed source behind the very text New Age culture quotes as free-floating spiritual psychology.

dossier.essence: A late-twentieth-century American metaphysical current in which discarnate intelligences are held to speak or write through entranced humans: Jane Roberts's 'Seth' (from 1963), Helen Schucman's inner dictation published as 'A Course in Miracles' (scribed 1965–72), and JZ Knight's 'Ramtha' (from 1977) are the type cases. Descended from nineteenth-century Spiritualist mediumship but shifted from contacting the dead to receiving book-length metaphysical curricula, it supplied the New Age movement's core doctrine: you create your own reality.
dossier.cosmos: Consciousness is primary and matter its projection — Seth calls the physical world a 'camouflage' constructed by belief; ACIM calls it an illusion born of the ego's dream of separation from God. The self is a local projection of a larger multidimensional entity or oversoul living many lives simultaneously; time is not linear; death is a change of focus rather than an ending, with reincarnation across probable realities. There is no hell and no external judgment — experience is understood as self-selected for the soul's education.
dossier.practice: Largely unchurched and book-mediated: study groups (ACIM's 365-lesson Workbook makes it literally a curriculum), workshops and recorded sessions with channels, and private belief-auditing and affirmation. The main institutional exception is Ramtha's School of Enlightenment (Yelm, Washington, founded 1988), which runs residential disciplines. What the current asks of adherents is cognitive: examine and revise beliefs, since experience is treated as their printout.
dossier.canon: Jane Roberts, 'The Seth Material' (1970), 'Seth Speaks' (1972), and 'The Nature of Personal Reality' (1974); 'A Course in Miracles' (1976); and Ramtha's dictated teachings, collected as 'Ramtha: The White Book'.
dossier.reflects: Revelation privatized and ongoing — scripture production relocated from ancient prophets to the entranced individual, with authority resting on psychological resonance rather than institutional warrant.
sources: Michael F. Brown, 'The Channeling Zone: American Spirituality in an Anxious Age' (1997)
Wouter J. Hanegraaff, 'New Age Religion and Western Culture' (1996)
J. Gordon Melton, 'Finding Enlightenment: Ramtha's School of Ancient Wisdom' (1998)
